top_items.bottom_line: This Swedish registry study found that only 1.4%–7.8% of witnessed out-of-hospital cardiac arrests (OHCA) would be eligible for extracorporeal cardiopulmonary resuscitation (ECPR), with eligibility highly dependent on selection criteria and transport times; this highlights the limited but significant population for whom ECPR could be considered in clinical practice.
top_items.title: Eligibility for Extracorporeal Cardio-Pulmonary Resuscitation in Out-of-Hospital Cardiac Arrests in Sweden.

top_items.bottom_line: This retrospective study suggests that preoperative tumor arterial embolization does not reduce intraoperative hemodynamic instability or blood loss overall in pediatric pheochromocytoma/paraganglioma surgery, but may help high-risk children with large or highly active tumors achieve outcomes similar to lower-risk patients; evidence is limited by small sample size and retrospective design, but findings may guide perioperative management in select high-risk pediatric cases.
top_items.title: Impact of Tumor Arterial Embolization on Hemodynamics During Surgical Resection of Pheochromocytoma and Paraganglioma in Children.

top_items.bottom_line: BOTTOM LINE: This article is an educational review summarizing current best practices, challenges, and strategies for tracheal intubation in head and neck surgery, emphasizing the importance of thorough preoperative evaluation, advanced airway techniques, and multidisciplinary collaboration, but does not present new clinical data or original research findings.
top_items.title: Tracheal intubation techniques in head and neck surgery: navigating challenges and enhancing airway safety

top_items.bottom_line: In this single-centre randomized controlled trial, extending individualised goal-directed fluid and blood pressure therapy into the postoperative period after oesophagectomy did not reduce postoperative morbidity compared to standard care, suggesting no clinical benefit to this more intensive approach despite increased fluid and vasopressor use.
top_items.title: Individualised Perioperative Blood Pressure and Fluid Therapy in Oesophagectomy a prospective, single-blind randomised controlled trial.

top_items.bottom_line: BOTTOM LINE: This multicenter, double-blind, placebo-controlled randomized controlled trial found that intercostal nerve block (ICNB) outperforms erector spinae plane block (ESPB) for reducing postoperative pain and opioid requirements in the crucial early period after uniportal video-assisted thoracoscopic surgery (VATS), without compromising safety.
top_items.title: Intercostal nerve block outperforms ESPB for uniportal VATS

top_items.bottom_line: BOTTOM LINE: The 2026 ASA Practice Guideline provides updated, evidence-based recommendations for perioperative pain management in adults and children undergoing cardiothoracic, mastectomy, and abdominal surgeries, strongly endorsing fascial plane blocks for many procedures to reduce postoperative pain and opioid use, though evidence quality varies from moderate to low depending on procedure and population.
top_items.title: New ASA recommendations for surgical pain relief

top_items.bottom_line: BOTTOM LINE: The SOFA trial, a single-center randomized controlled study published in 2024, compared opioid-free anesthesia (using agents like ketamine, lidocaine, clonidine, and magnesium sulfate) to standard opioid-based anesthesia in major surgery, aiming to assess whether opioid-free approaches improve early postoperative quality of recovery and reduce opioid-related complications.
top_items.title: Opioid-free anesthesia in major surgery

top_items.bottom_line: This article announces that the European Society of Anaesthesiology and Intensive Care (ESAIC) will adopt the GRADE methodology for guideline development starting in 2025, aiming to improve transparency, rigor, and clinical applicability; this transition is expected to enhance the quality and trustworthiness of future ESAIC guidelines, which is clinically important for informed decision-making.
top_items.title: Methodology series of ESAIC guidelines: standards in guidelines production.

top_items.bottom_line: In this large multicenter retrospective cohort study of over 778,000 deliveries, maternal peripartum cardiac arrest occurred at a rate of 11.2 per 100,000 deliveries, most commonly due to hemorrhage or amniotic fluid embolism, with higher risk in patients with advanced age, high BMI, certain racial backgrounds, and specific clinical conditions; these findings provide updated incidence and risk factor data to inform clinical vigilance and preparedness.
top_items.title: Frequency and Management of Maternal Peripartum Cardiac Arrest: A Multicenter Retrospective Cohort Analysis.

top_items.bottom_line: This systematic review and meta-analysis found that preoperative frailty, as measured by validated instruments, is common in older adults undergoing major cardiac procedures and is strongly associated with increased risks of postoperative complications, longer hospital stays, nonhome discharge, readmission, and higher short- and long-term mortality, underscoring the importance of frailty assessment in preoperative risk stratification for this population.
top_items.title: Association of Preoperative Frailty and Postoperative Outcomes in Older Adults Undergoing Major Cardiac Procedures: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis.

top_items.bottom_line: This large, prospective multicentre UK study found that known or predicted difficult airways are common in ICU patients, but most at-risk individuals are not clearly identified or do not have airway management plans, highlighting a significant gap in airway safety practices that warrants urgent clinical attention.
top_items.title: Identification of difficult airways in critical care units (ID-ACCT): a multicentre, survey-based prospective observational study.

top_items.bottom_line: BOTTOM LINE: A new prospective study indicates that radiofrequency ablation for lumbar facet-mediated low back pain not only reduces pain but also significantly improves walking speed, providing objective evidence that RFA enhances functional mobility in patients with lumbar spinal arthritis beyond traditional subjective pain assessments.
top_items.title: Radiofrequency ablation boosts mobility in lumbar spinal arthritis

top_items.bottom_line: BOTTOM LINE: This article provides an educational overview of discussions at a NYSORA event, highlighting the importance of multimodal, non-opioid pain management strategies in cardiac and thoracic surgery, but does not present new clinical data or study results.
top_items.title: Industry Spotlight at NYSORA Events: Advancing Non-Opioid Pain Management in Cardiac and Thoracic Surgery

top_items.bottom_line: This small pilot study found that prolonged remimazolam infusion (≥24 h) in both surgical and medical ICU patients resulted in predictable, stable pharmacokinetics with no significant differences between groups, suggesting remimazolam may be suitable for extended sedation in critically ill patients, though larger studies are needed to confirm safety and efficacy.
top_items.title: Prolonged infusion of remimazolam in surgical and medical intensive care unit patients: a pilot pharmacokinetic study.

top_items.bottom_line: In this single-centre randomized controlled trial of 100 oesophagectomy patients, extending goal-directed fluid therapy with individualized blood pressure targets into the postoperative period increased fluid and vasopressor use but did not reduce postoperative complications at 30 days, suggesting no clinical benefit to this approach over standard care.
top_items.title: Individualised Perioperative Blood Pressure and Fluid Therapy in Oesophagectomy a prospective, single-blind randomised controlled trial.

top_items.title: Industry Spotlight at NYSORA Events: Advancing Non-Opioid Pain Management in Cardiac and Thoracic Surgery

top_items.bottom_line: In this randomized clinical trial of children undergoing adenotonsillectomy, the presence of both parents during the perioperative period modestly reduced preoperative anxiety compared to the presence of only one parent, without affecting postoperative delirium or parental anxiety; this suggests that allowing both parents may be beneficial for pediatric patient comfort.
top_items.title: The Impact of the Presence of Both Parents on Perioperative Anxiety in Children Undergoing Adenotonsillectomy Surgery: A Randomized Clinical Trial.

top_items.title: Tracheal intubation techniques in head and neck surgery: navigating challenges and enhancing airway safety

top_items.title: Opioid-free anesthesia in major surgery

top_items.title: Frequency and Management of Maternal Peripartum Cardiac Arrest: A Multicenter Retrospective Cohort Analysis.

top_items.title: Radiofrequency ablation boosts mobility in lumbar spinal arthritis

top_items.bottom_line: This small pilot study found that prolonged remimazolam infusion (≥24 h) in both surgical and medical ICU patients resulted in predictable, stable pharmacokinetics without significant differences between groups, suggesting remimazolam may be suitable for extended sedation in critically ill patients, though larger studies are needed to confirm safety and efficacy.
top_items.title: Prolonged infusion of remimazolam in surgical and medical intensive care unit patients: a pilot pharmacokinetic study.

top_items.bottom_line: This large, multi-country post-hoc analysis found that differences in postoperative pulmonary complication rates among urological, gastrointestinal, and gynaecological abdominal surgeries are largely explained by patient factors and anaesthesia duration rather than surgical specialty itself, suggesting risk assessment should focus on these modifiable factors rather than the type of abdominal surgery alone.
top_items.channel: PubMed: Anaesthesia, Critical Care & Pain Medicine
top_items.title: Pulmonary Complications Following Urological, Gastrointestinal and Gynaecological Abdominal Surgery--a post-hoc analysis of an observational study in 29 countries.

⭐ Parturients' Perspectives on Labor Pain and Epidural Analgesia: An Explorative Qualitative Study. — This qualitative study highlights that epidural analgesia provides significant psychological as well as physical relief

top_items.bottom_line: In this large retrospective study of adults with major burns, early vasopressor use was associated with significantly higher rates of acute kidney injury, sepsis, and 30-day mortality even after propensity matching, suggesting caution with vasopressor initiation during acute burn resuscitation due to potential adverse outcomes.
top_items.title: Vasopressor Use in Acute Burn Resuscitation: A Retrospective Study.

top_items.bottom_line: This qualitative study highlights that epidural analgesia provides significant psychological as well as physical relief during labor, but individualized discussions about pain management goals are often lacking; clinicians should address both physical and psychological needs in antenatal and intrapartum care to improve patient-centered outcomes.
top_items.title: Parturients' Perspectives on Labor Pain and Epidural Analgesia: An Explorative Qualitative Study.

top_items.bottom_line: This systematic review and meta-analysis of 35 studies (including 23 clinical trials) found that Enhanced Recovery After Surgery (ERAS) protocols in low- and middle-income countries significantly improve short- and intermediate-term postoperative outcomes, supporting their implementation to reduce surgical complications and improve recovery in these settings.
top_items.title: Evidence on Enhanced Recovery After Surgery Protocols in Low- and Middle-Income Countries: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis of Postoperative Outcomes.

top_items.bottom_line: BOTTOM LINE: In a large retrospective study of over 2.5 million total hip and knee arthroplasty procedures from 2006–2021, neuraxial anesthesia was associated with significantly lower odds of major complications—including acute renal failure, blood transfusion, pneumonia, cardiac and pulmonary issues—as well as reduced ICU admissions and mechanical ventilation compared to general anesthesia.
top_items.title: Neuraxial vs general anesthesia in joint replacement

top_items.bottom_line: BOTTOM LINE: In patients undergoing total knee arthroplasty, adding a popliteal plexus block to a femoral triangle block provided modest reductions in early postoperative opioid consumption compared to femoral triangle block or adductor canal block alone, while preserving motor strength, according to the first randomized evidence evaluating this combined motor-sparing analgesic strategy.
top_items.title: Popliteal plexus block + femoral triangle block after TKA: Modest opioid savings with preserved strength

top_items.bottom_line: BOTTOM LINE: A newly published study in Pain Medicine (2025) reports that percutaneous laser disc decompression (PLDD), a minimally invasive procedure using focused laser energy to reduce disc pressure and size, provides significant structural and symptomatic relief for patients with lumbar disc herniation who have not responded to conservative treatments.
top_items.title: Percutaneous laser disc decompression offers significant relief for lumbar disc herniation

top_items.bottom_line: BOTTOM LINE: A decline in arterial blood pressure after surgery during the transition from the operating room to the ICU is associated with an increased risk of mortality and organ injury.
top_items.title: Blood Pressure Drop Between OR and ICU Mortality Risk

top_items.bottom_line: BOTTOM LINE: In mechanically ventilated adult ICU patients, a structured voice reorientation intervention using scripted family voice recordings, compared to usual care, was studied for its effect on reducing delirium; however, based solely on this excerpt, specific study results or conclusions regarding efficacy are not provided.
top_items.title: Delirium Reduction via Scripted Family Voice Recordings in Critically Ill Patients Receiving Mechanical Ventilation

top_items.bottom_line: BOTTOM LINE: The 2025 Perioperative Resuscitation and Life Support (PeRLS) guideline, developed by international experts using the GRADE methodology, provides updated, evidence-based recommendations tailored to the unique, highly monitored, and rapidly managed context of perioperative cardiac arrest, emphasizing timely diagnosis, targeted intervention, and context-specific resuscitation algorithms for anesthesiologists and critical care specialists.
top_items.title: Perioperative resuscitation and life support (PeRLS) 2025: redefining perioperative cardiac arrest management

top_items.bottom_line: Remimazolam appears to be as effective and safe as propofol or midazolam for ICU sedation, with favorable hemodynamic stability and no increased delirium or mortality risk, based on evidence from randomized trials, meta-analyses, and observational studies; its predictable recovery profile may aid ventilator weaning, making it a promising option for critically ill patients, though further ICU-specific research is warranted.
top_items.title: Safety and efficacy of remimazolam in critical illness.

top_items.bottom_line: In this large nationwide cohort study from South Korea, over half of ICU survivors developed post-intensive care syndrome (PICS) within 12 months, with risk factors including older age, female sex, lower income, pre-existing disability, comorbidities, and ICU interventions; these robust population-level data highlight the high incidence and key predictors of PICS, underscoring the need for targeted post-ICU care and monitoring.
top_items.title: Post-intensive care syndrome after critical illness: incidence and predictors in a nationwide cohort.

top_items.bottom_line: This large retrospective cohort study found that postoperative delirium detected in the PACU is strongly and independently associated with a higher risk of subsequent ward delirium, in-hospital falls, and 30-day mortality in older surgical patients, highlighting the clinical importance of early delirium screening and intervention in the immediate postoperative period.
top_items.title: Association between postoperative delirium in the postanaesthesia care unit and subsequent ward delirium: A retrospective cohort study.

top_items.bottom_line: In this prospective cohort study of post-cardiac surgery patients, greater end-expiratory lung volume loss and higher incidence of pendelluft during T-piece spontaneous breathing trials (SBT) were associated with SBT failure, suggesting that monitoring lung volume changes with electrical impedance tomography may help identify patients at risk for weaning failure; however, these findings require further validation before changing clinical practice.
top_items.title: Effects of two ventilator-weaning methods on lung volume and ventilation distribution by electrical impedance tomography in post-cardiac surgery patients: a prospective cohort study.

top_items.bottom_line: These 2024 guidelines provide 32 evidence-graded recommendations for emergency adult intubation outside the operating room and ICU, with 5 based on high-quality evidence and 12 on low-quality evidence, offering clinicians structured, consensus-based guidance for these high-risk scenarios.
top_items.channel: PubMed: Anaesthesia, Critical Care & Pain Medicine
top_items.title: Guidelines 2024: Emergency intubation of an adult outside the operating room and intensive care unit.

top_items.bottom_line: This study provides a systematically translated and culturally adapted Danish version of the FS-ICU-24R family satisfaction questionnaire, with face and content validity confirmed through cognitive interviews, enabling its appropriate use for assessing ICU family satisfaction in Denmark.
top_items.title: Translation and Cultural Adaption of the Revised ICU Family Satisfaction Questionnaire (FS-ICU-24R) Into Danish.

top_items.bottom_line: BOTTOM LINE: A 2026 randomized controlled noninferiority trial found that continuous posterior extrathoracic fascial plane block (PEFB) was noninferior to continuous paravertebral block (PVB) in reducing opioid consumption for trauma patients with multiple rib fractures, providing important evidence for effective pain management strategies in this population.
top_items.title: PEFB vs PVB for rib fracture pain

top_items.bottom_line: In this large multicenter RCT, maintaining maternal systolic blood pressure within 90% (vs. 80%) of baseline using noradrenaline during caesarean delivery under spinal anaesthesia reduced the incidence of neonatal acidosis and maternal hypotension without adverse effects, supporting a higher blood pressure target for improved neonatal and maternal outcomes.
top_items.title: Effect on neonatal outcomes of maintenance of maternal blood pressure targets with noradrenaline after spinal anaesthesia for caesarean delivery: a multicentre, randomised, controlled trial.

top_items.bottom_line: In this multicenter randomized controlled trial of 442 ICU patients, fiberoptic bronchoscopy guidance during percutaneous dilatational tracheostomy did not reduce perioperative complications compared to the nonendoscopic approach, but was associated with higher airway pressures and CO2 levels; these findings suggest routine bronchoscopy guidance may not be necessary in low-risk patients when performed by experienced clinicians.
top_items.title: Fiberoptic Bronchoscopy Monitoring During Percutaneous Dilatational Tracheostomy: A Multicenter, Randomized, Controlled Trial. The FIBERTRACH Randomized Clinical Trial.

top_items.bottom_line: BOTTOM LINE: In a 2026 randomized clinical trial of 80 elderly patients undergoing major orthopedic surgery with regional anesthesia, dexmedetomidine significantly reduced postoperative delirium, emergence delirium, early postoperative cognitive dysfunction, improved sleep quality, reduced long-term neuropathic pain, and provided greater cognitive stability over three months compared to propofol.
top_items.title: Dexmedetomidine reduces delirium and improves recovery after surgery

top_items.bottom_line: BOTTOM LINE: Rates of medication for opioid use disorder (MOUD) are increasing among surgical patients as hospitals adjust to changing pain management needs, according to recent trends highlighted in this report; however, no new clinical trial data or outcomes are presented in the provided excerpt.
top_items.title: MOUD Rates Rising Among Surgical Patients

top_items.bottom_line: This multicenter observational study found that near-normal cortical EEG activity can be restored during prolonged in-hospital cardiac arrest if specific cerebral oxygenation thresholds are met, and such restoration is associated with higher odds of return of spontaneous circulation; these findings suggest that brain viability may persist longer than previously assumed, with potential implications for resuscitation strategies, though evidence strength is limited by sample size.
top_items.title: Relationship between Cortical Electrical Responsiveness and Changes in Regional Cerebral Oxygenation (rSO2) and Return of Spontaneous Circulation in Prolonged Cardiac Arrest: A Multi-Center Observational Study.

top_items.bottom_line: This prospective cohort study found that the PROMIS-29 questionnaire demonstrates good content validity, internal consistency, construct validity, and responsiveness for measuring recovery after colorectal surgery, supporting its use as a reliable patient-reported outcome measure in perioperative care. This evidence strengthens the clinical utility of PROMIS-29 for tracking patient recovery and guiding postoperative management.
top_items.title: Psychometric Assessment of PROMIS-29 as a Measure of Recovery After Colorectal Surgery: A Prospective Cohort Study.

top_items.bottom_line: This prospective cohort study found that critically ill COVID-19 patients requiring early mechanical ventilation often have high pulmonary SARS-CoV-2 RNA loads and active viral replication, along with suppressed innate immune responses, suggesting that poor control of viral replication may contribute to rapid respiratory deterioration; these findings highlight the potential importance of early antiviral strategies in severe COVID-19.
top_items.channel: PubMed: Anaesthesia, Critical Care & Pain Medicine
top_items.title: Poor control of pulmonary viral replication in COVID-19 patients with early respiratory failure.

top_items.bottom_line: This large retrospective cohort study found that the probability of survival to hospital discharge in pediatric out-of-hospital cardiac arrest declines rapidly with longer EMS-initiated prehospital CPR duration, dropping below 1% after approximately 15 minutes; this evidence supports considering termination of resuscitation efforts after this time frame in most cases.
top_items.title: Duration of emergency medical services-initiated prehospital cardiopulmonary resuscitation efforts and survival for pediatric patients with out-of-hospital cardiac arrest.

top_items.bottom_line: BOTTOM LINE: This review highlights emerging evidence that challenges the routine use of bupivacaine and epinephrine in peripheral nerve blocks, emphasizing that bupivacaine may carry higher risks of neurotoxicity and myotoxicity compared to alternatives like ropivacaine or levobupivacaine, particularly in vulnerable patient populations, and calls for re-evaluation of standard pharmacologic choices.
top_items.title: Rethinking local anesthetic and vasopressor choices in peripheral nerve blocks: evidence-based updates for clinical practice

top_items.bottom_line: BOTTOM LINE: A 2026 meta-analysis of 36 randomized controlled trials involving 3,456 neonates found that regional anesthesia (spinal, epidural, or combined spinal–epidural) for cesarean delivery is associated with different neonatal outcomes compared to general anesthesia, highlighting the importance of anesthetic technique selection in optimizing neonatal health.
top_items.title: Neonatal Outcomes with Regional vs General Anesthesia for Cesarean Delivery

top_items.bottom_line: This commentary highlights that overreliance on quantitative productivity metrics in academic anesthesiology risks faculty burnout and diminished creativity, suggesting that departments should prioritize values like mentorship and community engagement to sustain innovation and well-being; while not primary research, it offers a conceptual framework with practical recommendations relevant for academic leadership.
top_items.title: Rewarding Productivity, Hoping for Creativity.

top_items.bottom_line: BOTTOM LINE: This anatomical cadaveric study quantified the mediolateral and craniocaudal spread of injectate and its contact with intercostal nerves after single and double superficial parasternal intercostal plane (SPIP) block injections, providing rare anatomical insight into the actual functional coverage clinicians may expect from this widely used block for sternotomy analgesia.
top_items.title: How far does a superficial parasternal block really spread?

⭐ Severity of Illness and Mortality According to Time of Admission to Intensive Care. — In this large Finnish ICU registry study, patients admitted in the morning had higher illness severity and in-hospital m

top_items.bottom_line: This systematic review and meta-analysis of 18 RCTs suggests that continuous glucose monitoring in ICU patients may reduce mortality and hypoglycemia compared to intermittent monitoring, but the evidence is of very low certainty, highlighting the need for larger, high-quality trials before changing clinical practice.
top_items.title: Effects of Continuous Versus Intermittent Glucose Monitoring in Intensive Care Unit Patients: A Systematic Review With Meta-Analysis.

top_items.bottom_line: In this large Finnish ICU registry study, patients admitted in the morning had higher illness severity and in-hospital mortality than those admitted at other times, even after adjustment for confounders, suggesting that delayed ICU referral overnight may worsen outcomes; this highlights the need for improved overnight recognition and timely escalation of care.
top_items.title: Severity of Illness and Mortality According to Time of Admission to Intensive Care.

top_items.bottom_line: This substudy of the AID-ICU trial developed and internally validated robust prediction models for 90-day and 1-year mortality in delirious ICU patients using pre-admission functional status and other baseline variables, achieving good discrimination (AUC 0.74) and calibration; incorporating functional status may improve risk stratification and guide clinical decision-making in this population.
top_items.title: Mortality Predictions Including Pre-Admission Functional Status in ICU Patients With Delirium-A Substudy of the AID-ICU Trial.

top_items.bottom_line: BOTTOM LINE: This article summarizes recent international guidelines recommending universal implementation of videolaryngoscopy as the standard device for tracheal intubation in both elective and emergency settings, highlighting its advantages over direct laryngoscopy in improving visualization, intubation success rates, and procedural safety, but does not present new clinical trial data.
top_items.title: Guidelines on strategies for the universal implementation of videolaryngoscopy

top_items.bottom_line: BOTTOM LINE: This article provides an educational overview of modern thoracic anesthesia, summarizing current strategies, pharmacologic options, and decision-making considerations for anesthetic induction and maintenance, but does not present new clinical data or original research findings.
top_items.title: Navigating modern thoracic anesthesia: strategies, drugs, and decisions

top_items.bottom_line: In this large multicenter cohort study of 852 liver transplant recipients, neither intraoperative fluid balance nor vasopressor dose was significantly associated with early graft dysfunction or primary graft non-function, suggesting that variations in these hemodynamic management strategies may not substantially impact short-term graft outcomes. This evidence may inform perioperative management protocols and reduce concerns about moderate differences in intraoperative fluid or vasopressor use during liver transplantation.
top_items.title: Intraoperative hemodynamic management during liver transplantation and postoperative morbidity: a multicenter cohort study.

top_items.bottom_line: This large, prospective UK study found that while most day-case surgery patients were satisfied with their pain relief, a significant proportion continued opioid use at day 7 and nearly 1 in 5 sought further help for pain, highlighting ongoing challenges in optimizing postoperative analgesia and opioid stewardship in ambulatory surgery.
top_items.title: Patient-reported outcomes, postoperative pain and pain relief after day-case surgery (POPPY): short-term peri-operative analgesic use.

top_items.bottom_line: This article highlights the lack of anesthesiology-specific research on human trafficking and argues that anesthesiologists, especially in labor and delivery settings, are likely to encounter trafficking victims, urging the specialty to engage in detection, intervention, and scholarship; while not presenting new empirical evidence, it raises important clinical awareness for anesthesiology practice.
top_items.title: Anti-Human Trafficking in Health Care: An Invitation to Anesthesiology.

top_items.bottom_line: High-flow nasal oxygen (HFNO) is increasingly supported by evidence as a safe and effective perioperative oxygenation strategy that improves oxygenation, prolongs safe apnoea time, and reduces desaturation compared to conventional oxygen therapy, particularly in high-risk surgical patients; however, clinical heterogeneity and unanswered questions about optimal use remain, highlighting the need for further research to guide personalized application.
top_items.channel: PubMed: Anaesthesia, Critical Care & Pain Medicine
top_items.title: High-Flow Nasal Oxygen in the Perioperative Setting: A Narrative Review.

top_items.bottom_line: BOTTOM LINE: This 2026 review highlights that optimal perioperative care for geriatric patients requires thorough frailty assessment, shared decision-making, and age-adapted strategies to address increased vulnerability due to comorbidities and reduced physiological reserve, with frailty being a stronger predictor of surgical risk than chronological age alone.
top_items.title: Perioperative care for geriatric patients

top_items.bottom_line: This large multicenter retrospective cohort study found that both low and high ionized calcium levels during weaning from cardiopulmonary bypass were associated with increased risk of postoperative low cardiac output syndrome, suggesting that maintaining iCa within a standard range may help reduce this complication; however, causality cannot be established due to the study design.
top_items.title: Association Between Ionized Calcium Concentrations During Weaning From Cardiopulmonary Bypass and Postoperative Low Cardiac Output Syndrome: A Retrospective Cohort Study.

top_items.bottom_line: BOTTOM LINE: This educational article discusses the significant role of perioperatively acquired muscle weakness in contributing to postoperative fatigue, highlighting their prevalence, impact on recovery and quality of life, and the need for greater clinical attention, but does not present new clinical trial data or original research findings.
top_items.title: The role of perioperatively acquired muscle weakness in postoperative fatigue

top_items.bottom_line: BOTTOM LINE: This article reviews current evidence on intraoperative methadone, noting that while some randomized controlled trials and meta-analyses suggest it may improve postoperative analgesia and reduce opioid consumption compared to conventional opioids, other studies report only modest or context-dependent benefits, highlighting both the promise and limitations of this approach in perioperative pain management.
top_items.title: Intraoperative methadone: promise and pitfalls in perioperative pain management

top_items.bottom_line: BOTTOM LINE: A newly published randomized controlled trial provides the first direct comparison of intra-articular platelet-rich plasma (PRP) versus corticosteroid injections for cervical facetogenic pain, evaluating differences in pain relief, functional improvement, and safety; clinicians should review the full study for specific outcomes and clinical implications.
top_items.title: Platelet-rich plasma vs corticosteroid injections for cervical facetogenic pain

⭐ Fast Antimicrobial Susceptibility Testing for Gram-Negative Bacteremia: The FAST Randomized Clinical Trial. — In this multicenter randomized trial, rapid phenotypic antimicrobial susceptibility testing for gram-negative bacteremia

top_items.bottom_line: This systematic review found low- to very low-certainty evidence that supraglottic airway devices do not improve survival or neurological outcomes compared to bag-valve-mask ventilation when used by basic life support providers during adult cardiac arrest, suggesting no clear clinical benefit for routine SGA use in this context.
top_items.title: Airway management with a supraglottic airway device during resuscitation by basic life support providers: a systematic review.

top_items.bottom_line: In this multicenter randomized trial, rapid phenotypic antimicrobial susceptibility testing for gram-negative bacteremia did not significantly improve 30-day clinical outcomes compared to standard testing, suggesting that while faster results are achievable, their clinical benefit in high-resistance settings may be limited when robust stewardship is present.
top_items.title: Fast Antimicrobial Susceptibility Testing for Gram-Negative Bacteremia: The FAST Randomized Clinical Trial.

top_items.bottom_line: This well-conducted randomized controlled trial found that perioperative magnesium sulfate infusion did not reduce the incidence of postoperative atrial fibrillation in cardiac surgery patients, indicating that routine prophylactic magnesium supplementation for POAF prevention is not supported.
top_items.title: Magnesium Sulfate to Prevent Perioperative Atrial Fibrillation in Cardiac Surgery: A Randomized Clinical Trial.

top_items.bottom_line: In this multicenter phase II randomized crossover trial of 54 ICU patients, a novel restraint device showed similar patient activity, safety, and satisfaction compared to traditional wrist restraints, suggesting it may be a viable alternative, though larger studies are needed to confirm these findings. This matters clinically as it offers a potential option to reduce reliance on traditional physical restraints without compromising patient safety or satisfaction.
top_items.title: Reducing Physical Restraint in the ICU: Multicenter Phase II Randomized Trial of a Novel Device Versus Traditional Wrist Restraints.

top_items.bottom_line: In this small, single-center randomized controlled trial, pre-emptive haptoglobin therapy guided by serum-free hemoglobin did not significantly reduce postoperative acute kidney injury compared to standard care in cardiac surgery patients, and the study was stopped early due to safety concerns; thus, routine pre-emptive haptoglobin use for AKI prevention in this setting is not supported.
top_items.title: Influence of Pre-emptive Haptoglobin on Postoperative Acute Kidney Injury in Cardiac Surgical Patients: A Randomized Controlled Trial.

top_items.bottom_line: This systematic review and meta-analysis of 16 RCTs (n=3278) found that the risk of post-dural puncture headache with dural puncture epidural or combined spinal-epidural using 24- or 25-gauge needles is very low and only slightly higher than with conventional epidural labor analgesia, suggesting that concerns about increased headache risk with these techniques and needle sizes may be overstated in clinical practice.
top_items.title: Post-dural Puncture Headache in Dural Puncture Epidural and Combined Spinal-Epidural Using 24- and 25-Gauge Needles Versus Conventional Epidural Labor Analgesia: A Systematic Review and Meta-analysis.

top_items.bottom_line: In this multicenter cluster-randomized trial in children with Chiari I malformation and syringomyelia, adding duraplasty to posterior fossa decompression did not significantly increase surgical complications but showed trends toward greater syrinx reduction and fewer repeat decompressions, though health-related quality of life was similar; larger studies are needed to clarify the clinical benefits and risks.
top_items.title: Decompression with or without Duraplasty for Chiari I and Syringomyelia.

top_items.bottom_line: In this randomized, double-blind trial of 384 women undergoing ambulatory surgery, preoperative olanzapine 5 mg (in addition to standard antiemetics) significantly improved quality of recovery and reduced postdischarge nausea through postoperative day 2 compared to placebo, without prolonging PACU stay, supporting its use for enhanced postoperative recovery in this population.
top_items.title: Preoperative Olanzapine and Quality of Recovery after Ambulatory Surgery: A Randomized Clinical Trial.

top_items.bottom_line: In this Danish registry-based study, community first responders (CFRs) frequently arrived before EMS and provided early CPR in pediatric out-of-hospital cardiac arrests, but about one-third experienced moderate or severe immediate distress, especially when arriving first, performing CPR, or assisting very young children; despite this, nearly all CFRs remained willing to continue volunteering, highlighting both the clinical benefit and psychological impact of CFR activation in pediatric emergencies.
top_items.title: Activation of Community First Responders to Pediatric Out-of-Hospital Cardiac Arrests in Denmark.

top_items.bottom_line: In this large MINT trial subanalysis, a restrictive transfusion strategy in patients with acute myocardial infarction and anemia—whether critically ill or not—did not significantly increase 30-day mortality or death/MI compared to a liberal strategy, suggesting restrictive transfusion thresholds are generally safe in this population. This supports the use of restrictive transfusion practices in AMI patients with anemia, including those in the ICU.
top_items.title: Red Cell Transfusion in Critically Ill and Noncritically Ill Patients With Acute Myocardial Infarction and Anemia-A Subanalysis of the Myocardial Ischemia and Transfusion (MINT) Trial.

top_items.bottom_line: This prospective observational study found that tactile stimulation in the delivery room is commonly used and is associated with a modest heart rate increase, particularly in newborns with initial HR <140 bpm, but those with bradycardia (HR <100 bpm for ≥10 seconds) in the first minute are four times more likely to require positive pressure ventilation, highlighting the importance of early heart rate assessment to guide resuscitation interventions.
top_items.title: Tactile Stimulation and Newborn Heart Rate Responses in the Delivery Room: An Observational Video Study.

top_items.bottom_line: This large retrospective study from New Zealand found no survival benefit after implementing double sequential defibrillation (DSD) for refractory VF/VT, with DSD patients showing lower odds of ROSC and 30-day survival—likely reflecting confounding by indication—highlighting that DSD should not be routinely adopted without further evidence.
top_items.title: Implementation of Double Sequential Defibrillation (DSD): An Aotearoa New Zealand observational study.

top_items.bottom_line: In this large, prospective, observational study, continuing metformin (but not SGLT2 inhibitors or GLP-1 receptor agonists) on the day of elective surgery in patients with type 2 diabetes was associated with a small but statistically significant increase in days alive and at home at 30 days, suggesting potential benefit to not withholding metformin perioperatively.
top_items.title: Withholding or continuing glucose-lowering drugs for elective surgery in patients with type 2 diabetes mellitus: a secondary analysis of the MOPED international, prospective, observational study.

top_items.bottom_line: BOTTOM LINE: This article is an educational case-based discussion on ECG interpretation in the context of a motor vehicle collision with suspected spinal cord injury, and does not present new clinical data or research findings.
top_items.title: ECG Pointers: MVC with Spinal Injury

top_items.bottom_line: BOTTOM LINE: This educational article reviews the causes, risk factors, identification, and potential complications of extravasation injuries in children, emphasizing the importance of early recognition and management but does not present new clinical data or research findings.
top_items.title: Extravasation injuries

⭐ Analgesic efficacy of locoregional anesthesia techniques in cardiac surgery: a systematic review and network meta-analysis of randomized trials. — This large network meta-analysis of 133 randomized trials found that intrathecal opioids, erector spinae plane block, an

top_items.bottom_line: This new American Thoracic Society guideline strongly recommends high-flow nasal cannula (HFNC) for acute hypoxemic respiratory failure and noninvasive ventilation (NIV) for acute hypercapnic respiratory failure in adults, based on systematic reviews and network meta-analyses showing reduced need for intubation and mortality; these evidence-based recommendations help clarify optimal noninvasive respiratory support strategies in acute respiratory failure.
top_items.title: Noninvasive Respiratory Support for Adult Patients with Acute Respiratory Failure. An Official American Thoracic Society Clinical Practice Guideline.

top_items.bottom_line: This large network meta-analysis of 133 randomized trials found that intrathecal opioids, erector spinae plane block, and parasternal intercostal plane block significantly reduce opioid consumption and pain after cardiac surgery, with intrathecal opioids providing the greatest pain relief; some techniques also shortened ICU and hospital stays, supporting their clinical use as effective alternatives or adjuncts to opioids.
top_items.title: Analgesic efficacy of locoregional anesthesia techniques in cardiac surgery: a systematic review and network meta-analysis of randomized trials.

top_items.bottom_line: In this large, multicenter randomized trial, high-dose intravenous vitamin C did not reduce 28-day mortality or persistent organ dysfunction in adults with severe burn injury and showed a trend toward possible harm, indicating it should not be used routinely in this population.
top_items.title: High-Dose Intravenous Vitamin C and Mortality and Organ Dysfunction in Severe Burn Injury: The VICTORY Randomized Clinical Trial.

top_items.bottom_line: This large, updated meta-analysis of 45 RCTs (32,215 patients) found no mortality benefit for intensive versus conventional glycemic control in critically ill adults, but intensive targets increased severe hypoglycemia risk and only showed subgroup benefits (e.g., neurologic outcomes in neuro ICU, mortality in cardiac surgery), with many findings limited by study bias and inconsistency; thus, routine intensive glycemic control is not broadly supported and should be individualized
top_items.title: Comparison of Intensive Versus Conventional Glycemic Control Targets: An Updated Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis of the 2024 Society of Critical Care Medicine Guidelines on Glycemic Control for Critically Ill Adults.

top_items.bottom_line: This systematic review and meta-analysis of 11 randomized controlled trials (2605 patients) found that immunomodulatory therapies after cardiac arrest did not significantly improve neurological outcomes or survival, with evidence limited by heterogeneity in interventions and timing; thus, current immunomodulation strategies should not be routinely used for post-cardiac arrest brain injury.
top_items.title: Effect of immunomodulation on post-cardiac arrest brain injury: a systematic review and meta-analysis.

top_items.bottom_line: In this randomized non-inferiority trial, intrathecal morphine did not achieve non-inferior analgesic efficacy compared to thoracic paravertebral block for patients undergoing video-assisted thoracoscopic surgery, with higher opioid consumption and more frequent rescue analgesia in the ITM group; these findings support continued preference for TPVB as the regional technique of choice for postoperative pain control in this setting.
top_items.title: Comparison of intrathecal morphine versus thoracic paravertebral block for video-assisted thoracoscopic surgery: a randomized non-inferiority trial.

top_items.bottom_line: This systematic review and meta-analysis found that the VExUS grading system shows moderate to good diagnostic accuracy for detecting elevated central venous and right atrial pressures, and is prognostically associated with acute kidney injury and mortality in cardiac patients (moderate to low certainty), but not in general critically ill populations; this suggests VExUS may be most clinically useful for risk stratification in cardiac patients rather than broadly in critical care.
top_items.title: Diagnostic and Prognostic Value of the Venous Excess Ultrasound Grading System: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis.

top_items.bottom_line: This large meta-analysis of randomized trials found that targeting higher intraoperative blood pressure during noncardiac surgery does not reduce acute kidney injury or myocardial injury compared to routine targets, with strong evidence supporting these findings; while there may be a reduction in postoperative delirium, the evidence is not yet sufficient to recommend higher targets for this outcome, suggesting current guideline thresholds remain appropriate.
top_items.title: Higher versus Routine Intraoperative Blood Pressure Targets in Noncardiac Surgery: A Systematic Review and Meta-analysis with Trial Sequential Analysis of Randomized Trials.

top_items.bottom_line: This moderate-certainty meta-analysis of 11 RCTs (n=5562) found that systemic prophylactic antibacterials in mechanically ventilated ICU patients probably reduce the incidence of VAP, but do not significantly impact mortality, duration of ventilation, or hospital stay, suggesting limited clinical benefit beyond VAP prevention.
top_items.title: Efficacy and Safety of Systemic Prophylactic Antibacterials in Mechanically Ventilated Patients: Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis.

top_items.bottom_line: This consensus report introduces standardized outcome measures (the resuscitation education Utstein style) for resuscitation education research, developed via a Delphi process, to enable consistent reporting and facilitate stronger evidence synthesis; its adoption is expected to improve the quality and comparability of educational research and ultimately enhance clinical practice.
top_items.title: Uniform reporting of outcomes from resuscitation education research: the resuscitation education Utstein style: a consensus report from the International Liaison Committee on Resuscitation.

top_items.bottom_line: In this large multicenter randomized trial, prone positioning in infants with moderate to severe acute bronchiolitis on high-flow nasal cannula did not reduce the need for escalation to noninvasive or invasive ventilation compared to supine positioning, indicating no clinical benefit for routine prone positioning in this setting.
top_items.title: Prone Positioning in Infants With Acute Bronchiolitis: The PROPOSITIS Randomized Clinical Trial.

top_items.bottom_line: In this multicenter, double-blind phase 2 RCT, early high-dose (10 g) intravenous vitamin C after out-of-hospital cardiac arrest did not reduce organ dysfunction at 96 hours and was associated with worse organ function, higher troponin release, and poorer neurological outcomes, indicating that high-dose vitamin C may be harmful in this setting.
top_items.title: Early high-dose vitamin C for out-of-hospital cardiac arrest: the VITaCCA randomized clinical trial.

top_items.bottom_line: In this large retrospective cohort study emulating a target trial, remimazolam did not reduce 30-day major adverse cardiovascular events or in-hospital mortality compared to propofol in older adults undergoing hip fracture surgery, suggesting no clinical advantage of remimazolam over propofol for these outcomes in this population.
top_items.title: Major Adverse Cardiovascular Events After Hip Fracture Surgery with Remimazolam or Propofol Total Intravenous Anesthesia: A Retrospective Cohort Study Emulating a Target Trial.

top_items.bottom_line: In this single-center randomized noninferiority trial of 360 critically ill patients with hypomagnesemia, enteral magnesium replacement did not meet noninferiority criteria compared to IV replacement for serum magnesium correction at 24 hours, though it was associated with lower costs, waste, and environmental impact; thus, while enteral replacement offers sustainability benefits, IV administration remains more effective for rapid correction in this setting.
top_items.title: Enteral When Compared With IV Magnesium Replacement in the Critically Ill: A Noninferiority Randomized Clinical Trial.

top_items.bottom_line: In this large, multicenter, cluster-randomized trial, prehospital transfusion with type O whole blood did not reduce 30-day mortality compared to blood component therapy in trauma patients with hemorrhagic shock, and storage age of whole blood had no impact on outcomes, suggesting no clinical advantage of whole blood over components in this setting.
top_items.title: Prehospital Resuscitation with Type O Whole Blood for Trauma and Hemorrhage.
